title: After a Bitter Split, European Leaders Play Nice With Trump
summary: A peace framework with Iran, and hope for cooperation with Ukraine, softened the tone on Tuesday at a Group of 7 gathering in France.

title: Who Are the Leaders at the G7 Summit in France?
summary: On a roll or against a wall, Group of 7 leaders bring sharply different agendas. The leaders of some other nations are also attending to press their own interests.

title: Can a Trump-Modi Meeting Reset U.S.-India Relations?
summary: Despite mutual praise between the two leaders, there has been a series of setbacks to U.S. relations with India since they last met more than a year ago.

title: The U.S. and Iran Tamp Down Heated Rhetoric
summary: Also, primary elections test Trump’s sway in the South. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.

title: Hillary Clinton Says Biden’s Re-election Bid Was a ‘Terrible Mistake’
summary: If the former president had “passed the torch” and allowed a competitive Democratic primary in 2024, she said in a new interview, the winner “would have beaten Donald Trump.”

title: Democrats Keep Helping Out This Far-Right Maryland Republican
summary: Gov. Wes Moore, a Democrat, has begun running ads on Fox News that promote Dan Cox, a Republican running in a contested primary to challenge him.

title: Obama’s Nuclear Deal Looms Over Trump’s Iran Negotiations
summary: President Trump is under pressure to significantly improve upon the Obama-era deal in order to justify the huge human and economic cost of taking the United States to war.

title: Republicans Again Block War Powers Measure in the Senate
summary: Four G.O.P. senators crossed party lines to back the resolution to direct President Trump to halt the war in Iran and seek authorization from Congress, but their support was not enough.

title: Six Takeaways From the Times Investigation Into Epstein’s Death
summary: We considered every plausible theory of his death, both official and otherwise, seeking out the most persuasive arguments and evidence for each.

title: The Big Questions About Jeffrey Epstein: What The Times Has Learned
summary: Here is what we know about the sexual predator whose secrets spurred an international reckoning over money, power and complicity. Check back for updates.

title: Why Is Everyone Waiting Hours for Frozen Yogurt? Why Am I?
summary: The yogurt is not special. Let’s just get that out of the way now.

title: As Mourning Begins, Deadly B-52 Crash Highlights Age of Bomber Fleet
summary: Investigators are just beginning to determine what went wrong, but the Air Force has struggled to maintain the planes, which were first introduced in 1955.

title: Internal Emails Raise Questions About D.H.S. Ending Haiti Protections
summary: Newly released documents could complicate deliberations by the Supreme Court over whether the Trump administration can immediately cancel deportation protections for some migrants.

title: Inquiry Into Justice Dept. Lawyer Ends Without Disciplinary Action
summary: A special counsel found that a government lawyer helped mislead a judge into releasing a man wanted for homicide abroad, but largely blamed the Trump administration.

title: Claiming an Antifa Plot, U.S. Charges 15 in Minneapolis With Conspiracy
summary: The conspiracy indictment was filed as federal prosecutors in Minnesota had trouble sustaining many criminal cases filed against Immigration and Customs Enforcement protesters.

title: Judge Orders Kennedy Center to Make a Plan for Staying Open
summary: As the Trump administration seeks to move forward with renovations at the center, a judge has asked for its programming calendar.

title: Vance Is Pressed on Epstein, Race and His Boss on ‘The View’
summary: The vice president also talked to Megyn Kelly on Tuesday about critics of the new Iran cease-fire and his presidential ambition.

title: Washington’s Reagan Airport Will Ground Flights for July 4 Events
summary: The runways at Ronald Reagan National Airport will be closed after noon on July 4, and for several hours on July 3. Other celebrations surrounding the nation’s 250th birthday are also expected to cause disruptions.

title: Ebola Outbreak Could Become Worst on Record, Africa C.D.C. Chief Warns
summary: Health workers have warned that the outbreak, already one of the worst in decades, could take as long as a year to contain if infection rates do not flatten.

title: Beijing’s New Message to Its Citizens: Your Money Belongs at Home
summary: Eager to keep capital within its borders, China is restricting the ways individuals can engage with global markets.

title: Are You a Morning Person or Night Owl? Take Our Sleep Quiz.
summary: Think you’re a morning person, a night owl or something in between? Take our “chronotype” quiz to find out.

title: Iran Found Trump’s Bone Spur
summary: In a contest of wills, the hard men of Tehran prevailed over the vain man of Washington.

title: Good Luck, Kevin Warsh. You’ll Need It.
summary: Between a surge in interest rates, increasingly restive colleagues and the demands of President Trump, the new Fed chairman, Kevin Warsh, is in a bind.

title: Tell Us Why Taylor Swift’s Wedding Is Meaningful to You
summary: For an upcoming story, we want fans to talk to us about the journey they have been on with Swift through the years and how they feel about her big day.

title: Gregory Williams, Academic With an Uncommon Perspective on Race, Dies at 81
summary: As a child, he discovered that his father — and therefore he and his siblings — had been passing for white. For the rest of his life, he identified as Black.
